examples.target: He had to {drag} the heavy box across the floor.
examples.source: Ağır kutuyu yerde sürüklemek zorunda kaldı.

examples.target: She {dragged} her suitcase up the stairs.
The child tried to {drag} a big toy wagon.
examples.source: Çocuk büyük bir oyuncak vagonu sürüklemeye çalıştı.

examples.target: Don't {drag} your feet when you walk.
We had to {drag} the fallen tree off the road.
examples.source: Devrilmiş ağacı yoldan sürüklemek zorunda kaldık.

examples.target: The dog tried to {drag} its bed to the corner.
He {dragged} the chair closer to the table.
It's hard to {drag} a boat out of the water alone.
examples.source: Bir tekneyi yalnız başına sudan çıkarmak zordur.

examples.target: She {dragged} the blanket into the living room.
They had to {drag} the broken car to the garage.
examples.source: Bozuk arabayı garaja sürüklemek zorunda kaldılar.

examples.target: The children love to {slide} down the hill in winter.
She watched the book {slide} off the shelf.
He tried to {slide} the door open quietly.
The glass began to {slide} toward the edge of the table.
examples.source: Bardak masanın kenarına doğru kaymaya başladı.

examples.target: Be careful — the floor is wet and you might {slide}.
examples.source: Dikkat et — zemin ıslak ve kayabilirsin.

examples.target: The drawer doesn't {slide} smoothly anymore.
Kids {slide} down the playground slide with joy.
examples.source: Çocuklar oyun parkı kaydırağından sevinçle kayarlar.

examples.target: She {slid} the letter under the door.
The coin {slid} across the countertop.
He {slid} into his seat just before class started.
examples.source: Ders başlamadan hemen önce yerine kaydı.

examples.target: The baby learned to {crawl} before she could walk.
examples.source: Bebek yürümeyi öğrenmeden önce emeklemeyi öğrendi.

examples.target: He had to {crawl} through the narrow tunnel.
examples.source: Dar tünelden geçmek için sürünmek zorunda kaldı.

examples.target: The soldiers were trained to {crawl} silently.
The cat began to {crawl} toward the mouse.
Traffic was so bad that cars had to {crawl} along.
examples.source: Trafik o kadar kötüydü ki arabalar sürünerek ilerliyordu.

examples.target: She had to {crawl} under the bed to find her ring.
examples.source: Yüzüğünü bulmak için yatağın altına sürünmek zorunda kaldı.

examples.target: The bug {crawled} slowly up the wall.
examples.source: Böcek duvarda yavaşça sürünerek yukarı çıktı.

examples.target: The child {crawled} out from under the table.
He {crawled} through the gap in the fence.
examples.source: Çitteki aralıktan geçmek için sürünerek geçti.

examples.target: After the accident, she could only {crawl} for a few days.
examples.source: Kazadan sonra birkaç günlüğüne sadece emekleyebildi.

examples.target: The sun will {rise} early tomorrow morning.
Prices tend to {rise} during the summer season.
She watched the balloon {rise} into the sky.
The water level began to {rise} after the rain.
examples.source: Yağmurdan sonra su seviyesi yükselmeye başladı.

examples.target: He {rose} from his chair to greet the guest.
Smoke began to {rise} from the chimney.
The temperature is expected to {rise} this weekend.
examples.source: Sıcaklığın bu hafta sonu yükselmesi bekleniyor.

examples.target: The dough will {rise} if you leave it long enough.
examples.source: Hamuru yeterince uzun süre bırakırsan kabaracak.

examples.target: She {rose} early every day to exercise.
examples.source: Her gün egzersiz yapmak için erken kalktı.

examples.target: The river {rose} dangerously after the storm.
examples.source: Nehir fırtınadan sonra tehlikeli bir şekilde yükseldi.

examples.target: Wood can {float} on water naturally.
examples.source: Tahta doğal olarak su üzerinde yüzebilir.

examples.target: The boat began to {float} downstream.
examples.source: Tekne aşağı akıntıya doğru yüzmeye başladı.

examples.target: She watched the leaves {float} on the surface of the lake.
Clouds seem to {float} across the sky on windy days.
examples.source: Rüzgarlı günlerde bulutlar gökyüzünde süzülüyormuş gibi görünür.

examples.target: The balloon {floated} gently over the town.
He loved to {float} on his back in the swimming pool.
The paper boat {floated} for a while before sinking.
examples.source: Kağıt tekne batmadan önce bir süre yüzdü.

examples.target: Dead fish {float} to the surface of the water.
Feathers {float} through the air very slowly.
She let her worries {float} away on the peaceful river.
examples.source: Endişelerini sakin nehirde süzülerek uzaklaşmasına izin verdi.

examples.target: Heavy objects tend to {sink} in water.
The ship began to {sink} after hitting a rock.
examples.source: Gemi bir kayaya çarptıktan sonra batmaya başladı.

examples.target: Her feet {sank} into the soft mud.
He watched his keys {sink} to the bottom of the pool.
The sun {sank} below the horizon at dusk.
Her heart {sank} when she heard the bad news.
The old building slowly {sank} into the swamp.
examples.source: Eski bina yavaş yavaş bataklığa battı.

examples.target: Metal usually {sinks} in water.
The diver watched the anchor {sink} into the deep water.
examples.source: Dalgıç demirin derin suya battığını izledi.

examples.target: If you don't fix the leak, the boat will {sink}.
She likes to {walk} to work every morning.
examples.source: Her sabah işe yürüyerek gitmeyi sever.

examples.target: He {walked} for hours without getting tired.
The doctor said I should {walk} at least 30 minutes a day.
examples.source: Doktor günde en az 30 dakika yürümem gerektiğini söyledi.

examples.target: Can you {walk} the dog this afternoon?
examples.source: Bu öğleden sonra köpeği gezdirebilir misin?

examples.target: They {walked} along the river and enjoyed the view.
examples.source: Nehir boyunca yürüyüp manzaranın tadını çıkardılar.

examples.target: It's faster to take the bus than to {walk}.
examples.source: Yürüyüş yapmak yerine otobüs almak daha hızlı.

examples.target: She {walked} slowly because her feet hurt.
He {walked} her to the door and said goodbye.
examples.source: Onu kapıya kadar götürdü ve veda etti.

examples.target: The children {walk} to school together every day.
examples.source: Çocuklar her gün birlikte okula yürürler.

examples.target: We decided to {walk} instead of taking a taxi.
Please {shake} the bottle before drinking.
The earthquake made the whole building {shake}.
She {shook} her head in disagreement.
He {shook} hands with everyone in the room.
The cold wind made her {shake} uncontrollably.
examples.source: Soğuk rüzgar onu kontrolsüz bir şekilde titretti.

examples.target: The dog {shook} the water off its fur after swimming.
examples.source: Köpek yüzdükten sonra tüylerindeki suyu silkti.

examples.target: She {shook} the tree to make the fruits fall.
His hands {shook} as he opened the letter.
The baby loves it when you gently {shake} the rattle.
examples.source: Bebek çıngırağı nazikçe salladığında çok seviyor.

examples.target: He could barely {shake} off the feeling of fear.
examples.source: Korku hissini üzerinden atmakta güçlük çekti.

examples.target: She had to stand on her toes to {reach} the top shelf.
examples.source: Üst rafa ulaşmak için parmak uçlarında durmak zorunda kaldı.

examples.target: We finally {reached} the top of the mountain.
Can you {reach} the remote control from where you're sitting?
examples.source: Oturduğun yerden uzaktan kumandaya uzanabilir misin?

examples.target: He tried to {reach} her by phone but got no answer.
examples.source: Onu telefonla aramaya çalıştı ama cevap alamadı.

examples.target: The vine {reached} all the way to the roof.
We need to {reach} an agreement by tomorrow.
examples.source: Yarına kadar bir anlaşmaya varmamız gerekiyor.

examples.target: The water {reached} her waist.
She {reached} into her bag to find her keys.
The road {reaches} the city after 10 kilometers.
He {reached} out and grabbed the falling cup.
Be careful not to {step} on the wet floor.
She {stepped} outside to get some fresh air.
examples.source: Biraz temiz hava almak için dışarı çıktı.

examples.target: He {stepped} on a nail and hurt his foot.
examples.source: Bir çiviye bastı ve ayağını yaraladı.

examples.target: Please {step} aside so others can pass.
examples.source: Diğerleri geçebilsin diye lütfen bir yana çekilin.

examples.target: She gently {stepped} over the sleeping cat.
He {stepped} forward to accept the prize.
examples.source: Ödülü almak için öne adım attı.

examples.target: I accidentally {stepped} on her foot in the crowd.
Don't {step} on the grass in the garden.
She {stepped} back in surprise when the door opened.
examples.source: Kapı açılınca şaşkınlıkla geri adım attı.

examples.target: He took a deep breath before {stepping} onto the stage.
examples.source: Sahneye çıkmadan önce derin bir nefes aldı.

examples.target: The ball {rolled} down the hill quickly.
He {rolled} the dough flat on the table.
examples.source: Hamuru masada düz bir şekilde açtı.

examples.target: The coin {rolled} under the sofa.
She {rolled} her sleeves up before washing the dishes.
The car {rolled} slowly into the parking spot.
Tears {rolled} down her cheeks.
He {rolled} over in his sleep.
The dog loves to {roll} in the grass.
She asked him to {roll} the carpet up.
The barrel {rolled} off the truck.
The top can {spin} for a very long time.
She {spun} around when she heard her name.
The wheels {spin} fast on a racing car.
He {spun} the globe to find the country.
The washing machine {spins} the clothes dry.
The dancer {spun} gracefully across the stage.
His head started to {spin} after the ride.
She {spun} the bottle during the game.
The fan {spins} to cool down the room.
He {spun} the coin on the table.
She {tossed} the ball to her friend.
He {tossed} his coat onto the chair.
Let's {toss} a coin to decide who goes first.
examples.source: Kimin önce gideceğine karar vermek için yazı tura atalım.

examples.target: She {tossed} the salad with olive oil.
He {tossed} and turned all night and couldn't sleep.
examples.source: Bütün gece döndü durdu ve uyuyamadı.

examples.target: The chef {tossed} the vegetables in the pan.
She {tossed} the letter into the bin without reading it.
He {tossed} his keys on the table as he walked in.
The waves {tossed} the small boat around.
examples.source: Dalgalar küçük tekneyi bir o yana bir bu yana çarptı.

examples.target: She {tossed} her hair back and smiled.
Please {knock} before you enter the room.
She {knocked} on the door three times.
He accidentally {knocked} the glass off the table.
Someone is {knocking} at the door — go see who it is.
She {knocked} her elbow on the wall.
He {knocked} the nail into the wall with a hammer.
The wind {knocked} over the flowerpot on the balcony.
She {knocked} lightly and waited for an answer.
He {knocked} down the old wall to make more space.
examples.source: Daha fazla alan açmak için eski duvarı yıktı.

examples.target: Who {knocked} all the books off the shelf?
She {waved} goodbye as the train left the station.
examples.source: Tren istasyondan ayrılırken el sallayarak veda etti.

examples.target: He {waved} at me from across the street.
The flag {waved} in the strong wind.
She {waved} her hand to get the waiter's attention.
examples.source: Garsonun dikkatini çekmek için elini salladı.

examples.target: The crowd {waved} their banners during the parade.
He {waved} the smoke away from his face.
examples.source: Dumanı yüzünden uzaklaştırmak için elini salladı.

examples.target: The children {waved} excitedly as the bus arrived.
examples.source: Otobüs gelince çocuklar heyecanla el salladı.

examples.target: She {waved} at her neighbor from the window.
The teacher {waved} for the students to be quiet.
examples.source: Öğretmen öğrencilerin sessiz olması için elini salladı.

examples.target: Palm trees {wave} gently in the breeze.
I have {already} eaten, so I'm not hungry.
examples.source: Zaten yedim, bu yüzden aç değilim.

examples.target: She {already} knew the answer before I explained.
examples.source: Ben açıklamadan önce cevabı çoktan biliyordu.

examples.target: Have you {already} finished your homework?
The train had {already} left when we arrived.
He {already} lives in a different city now.
examples.source: Artık zaten farklı bir şehirde yaşıyor.

examples.target: Is it {already} time to go home?
examples.source: Eve gitme zamanı geldi mi zaten?

examples.target: I {already} told you about this yesterday.
She's {already} seen that film twice.
examples.source: O filmi çoktan iki kez izledi.

examples.target: Don't buy more bread — we {already} have plenty.
examples.source: Daha fazla ekmek alma — çoktan yeterince var.

examples.target: They {already} know each other from school.
She is {still} waiting for a reply.
Is he {still} working at the same company?
I {still} remember that wonderful day.
The weather is {still} very cold today.
She is {still} learning English after five years.
examples.source: Beş yıl sonra hâlâ İngilizce öğreniyor.

examples.target: He {still} hasn't called back.
There is {still} some food in the fridge.
Are you {still} awake at this hour?
The children are {still} playing outside.
He {still} believes things will get better.
Has she arrived {yet}?
I haven't finished the report {yet}.
Don't leave {yet} — the show hasn't started.
Have you eaten {yet}?
He hasn't decided {yet} where to go.
The results are not ready {yet}.
I can't believe it's 9 o'clock {yet}.
Have you met him {yet}?
She hasn't replied to my message {yet}.
The package hasn't arrived {yet}.
Can you say that {again}? I didn't hear you.
She fell asleep and woke up {again} after an hour.
examples.source: Uyuyakaldı ve bir saat sonra yeniden uyandı.

examples.target: I would love to visit that place {again}.
examples.source: O yeri tekrar ziyaret etmek isterim.

examples.target: He failed the test but tried {again} the next day.
examples.source: Sınavdan kaldı ama ertesi gün tekrar denedi.

examples.target: She {again} forgot to lock the door.
The problem has come up {again}.
Say that {again} — you want to resign?
examples.source: Bir daha söyle — istifa etmek mi istiyorsun?

examples.target: They met {again} after many years.
The old computer stopped working {again}.
I hope we can work together {again} someday.
examples.source: Umarım bir gün yeniden birlikte çalışabiliriz.

examples.target: Have you seen him {lately}?
She hasn't been feeling well {lately}.
I've been very busy {lately} with work.
He has been eating healthier {lately}.
What have you been doing {lately}?
{Lately} I've had trouble sleeping at night.
examples.source: Son zamanlarda geceleri uyumakta güçlük çekiyorum.

examples.target: The weather has been really strange {lately}.
examples.source: Son zamanlarda hava gerçekten tuhaf davranıyor.

examples.target: Have you heard any good music {lately}?
examples.source: Son zamanlarda iyi müzik duydun mu?

examples.target: She's been smiling a lot more {lately}.
examples.source: Son zamanlarda çok daha fazla gülümsüyor.

examples.target: I haven't spoken to my old friend {lately}.
He {recently} moved to a new apartment.
examples.source: Yakın zamanda yeni bir daireye taşındı.

examples.target: I {recently} read a very interesting book.
examples.source: Geçenlerde çok ilginç bir kitap okudum.

examples.target: Have they {recently} updated the software?
She has {recently} started learning to cook.
examples.source: Yakın zamanda yemek pişirmeyi öğrenmeye başladı.

examples.target: I {recently} ran into an old classmate.
examples.source: Geçenlerde eski bir sınıf arkadaşımla karşılaştım.

examples.target: The company has {recently} hired more staff.
examples.source: Şirket yakın zamanda daha fazla personel aldı.

examples.target: The park was {recently} renovated.
She {recently} got married.
Have you {recently} tried the new café downtown?
examples.source: Şehir merkezindeki yeni kafe denemesini denedin mi?

examples.target: He was {recently} promoted at work.
She {finally} passed her driving test after four attempts.
examples.source: Dört denemeden sonra sonunda ehliyet sınavını geçti.

examples.target: The rain {finally} stopped after two days.
He {finally} apologized for his mistake.
They {finally} agreed on a price.
We {finally} found the hotel after getting lost.
The package {finally} arrived after three weeks.
examples.source: Paket üç haftanın ardından sonunda geldi.

examples.target: She {finally} understood what the teacher meant.
examples.source: Öğretmenin ne demek istediğini sonunda anladı.

examples.target: He {finally} finished building his house.
After hours of waiting, the doctor {finally} called her name.
examples.source: Saatlerce bekledikten sonra doktor nihayet adını çağırdı.

examples.target: They {finally} made a decision after much debate.
examples.source: Uzun tartışmalardan sonra nihayet bir karar verdiler.

examples.target: The lights went out {suddenly} during the storm.
She {suddenly} remembered where she had left her keys.
He {suddenly} stopped walking and looked back.
The dog {suddenly} started barking at nothing.
examples.source: Köpek hiçbir şeye karşı aniden havlamaya başladı.

examples.target: {Suddenly} the car in front of us stopped.
The weather changed {suddenly} and it began to rain.
examples.source: Hava aniden değişti ve yağmur yağmaya başladı.

examples.target: She {suddenly} felt very tired and sat down.
examples.source: Aniden çok yorgun hissetti ve oturdu.

examples.target: He {suddenly} understood the solution.
The phone rang {suddenly} in the quiet room.
The child {suddenly} burst into tears.
Please call me {immediately} if anything changes.
examples.source: Bir şey değişirse lütfen hemen ara.

examples.target: She {immediately} recognized his voice.
He left the office {immediately} after the meeting.
The doctor asked her to come in {immediately}.
I {immediately} noticed that something was wrong.
examples.source: Bir şeylerin ters gittiğini hemen fark ettim.

examples.target: She answered {immediately} without any hesitation.
examples.source: Hiç tereddüt etmeden hemen cevap verdi.

examples.target: The fire was put out {immediately}.
He {immediately} understood what I was trying to say.
{Immediately} after lunch, she went back to work.
examples.source: Öğle yemeğinin hemen ardından işe geri döndü.

examples.target: The teacher {immediately} stopped the class when the alarm rang.
examples.source: Alarm çalınca öğretmen dersi derhal durdurdu.

examples.target: She is {constantly} checking her phone.
He {constantly} asks questions in class.
The machine needs to be {constantly} monitored.
She was {constantly} worried about her health.
The baby was {constantly} crying all night.
He {constantly} forgets where he puts his glasses.
The wind was {constantly} blowing during the trip.
She is {constantly} improving her skills.
He {constantly} complained about the noise.
The river {constantly} changes its course over the years.
examples.source: Nehir yıllar içinde sürekli yatağını değiştirir.

examples.target: I {usually} have coffee in the morning.
She {usually} walks to work.
He is {usually} quiet during meetings.
We {usually} eat dinner at 7 o'clock.
It {usually} takes 20 minutes to get there.
She {usually} reads before going to sleep.
He {usually} helps his mother with the cooking.
examples.source: Genellikle annesine yemek yapmada yardım eder.

examples.target: Children {usually} enjoy playing outdoors.
The bus {usually} arrives on time.
She {usually} prefers tea over coffee.
examples.source: Genellikle kahve yerine çayı tercih eder.

examples.target: She {rarely} eats fast food.
He {rarely} misses a day of work.
I {rarely} see him anymore since he moved.
Snow {rarely} falls in this part of the country.
examples.source: Ülkenin bu bölgesinde nadiren kar yağar.

examples.target: She {rarely} complains about anything.
He {rarely} watches television.
Such opportunities {rarely} come twice.
examples.source: Bu tür fırsatlar nadiren iki kez gelir.

examples.target: I {rarely} cook during the week.
She {rarely} raises her voice.
They {rarely} disagree with each other.
The food here is {mostly} vegetarian.
He spends his free time {mostly} reading.
examples.source: Boş zamanını büyük ölçüde okuyarak geçiriyor.

examples.target: The audience was {mostly} young people.
The work is {mostly} done — just a few things left.
examples.source: İş büyük ölçüde bitti — sadece birkaç şey kaldı.

examples.target: She was {mostly} quiet during the conversation.
The trip was {mostly} enjoyable.
He is {mostly} interested in science.
The menu is {mostly} in English.
The children were {mostly} well-behaved.
I {mostly} agree with what you said.
She has {nearly} finished writing the report.
It is {nearly} midnight.
He was {nearly} hit by a car on the road.
I've {nearly} read all the books on that shelf.
examples.source: O raftaki kitapların neredeyse tamamını okudum.

examples.target: The project cost {nearly} twice the budget.
examples.source: Proje bütçenin neredeyse iki katına mal oldu.

examples.target: She was {nearly} in tears by the end of the film.
They have been friends for {nearly} twenty years.
The bag is {nearly} empty.
He {nearly} fell off his chair laughing.
We are {nearly} there — just five more minutes.
examples.source: Neredeyse geldik — sadece beş dakika daha.

examples.target: She could {barely} keep her eyes open.
He {barely} passed the exam.
I can {barely} hear you — speak louder.
examples.source: Seni zar zor duyabiliyorum — daha yüksek sesle konuş.

examples.target: She {barely} had time to eat breakfast.
examples.source: Kahvaltı yapacak zamanı zar zor buldu.

examples.target: The old man {barely} recognized his own house.
examples.source: Yaşlı adam kendi evini güçlükle tanıyabildi.

examples.target: There was {barely} enough food for everyone.
examples.source: Herkes için zar zor yetecek kadar yemek vardı.

examples.target: He {barely} slept the night before the big day.
examples.source: Büyük günden önceki gece güçlükle uyudu.

examples.target: She {barely} smiled when she heard the news.
The child {barely} reached the counter.
They {barely} made it to the station on time.
The lamp hangs {above} the dining table.
The temperature rose {above} 35 degrees yesterday.
Her score was {above} the class average.
The birds circled {above} the field.
A painting hung {above} the fireplace.
Put the box {above} the wardrobe.
He lives in the apartment {above} mine.
The clouds were far {above} us.
Sound travels faster {above} water than below it.
examples.source: Ses suyun üstünde altından daha hızlı yayılır.

examples.target: The stars shone {above} the quiet village.
The temperature dropped {below} zero last night.
examples.source: Dün gece sıcaklık sıfırın altına düştü.

examples.target: There is a small shop {below} the apartment.
examples.source: Dairenin altında küçük bir dükkan var.

examples.target: The fish swam {below} the surface of the water.
Her grades fell {below} the required level.
The valley was {below} the mountain peak.
He signed his name {below} the letter.
Please write your answer in the space {below}.
The map shows the route from {below} the bridge.
examples.source: Harita köprünün altından geçen rotayı gösteriyor.

examples.target: The cave was located {below} the cliff.
She hid the key {below} a large rock.
examples.source: Anahtarı büyük bir taşın altına sakladı.

examples.target: The cat is hiding {behind} the sofa.
She stood {behind} him in the queue.
Don't forget to check {behind} the door.
He fell {behind} the rest of the class.
There is a garden {behind} the house.
The truth was {behind} his strange behavior.
She left her worries {behind} when she went on holiday.
He was {behind} with his rent payment.
The children played {behind} the school building.
Who is {behind} this great project?
examples.source: Bu harika projenin arkasında kim var?

examples.target: She sat {beside} her best friend during the exam.
examples.source: Sınav sırasında en iyi arkadaşının yanına oturdu.

examples.target: He placed the vase {beside} the window.
A small café is located {beside} the park.
examples.source: Parkın yanında küçük bir kafe yer alıyor.

examples.target: She kept her phone {beside} her bed all night.
examples.source: Telefonunu bütün gece yatağının yanında tuttu.

examples.target: He knelt {beside} the injured dog.
The bookstore is {beside} the library.
She walked {beside} him all the way to the gate.
Leave your shoes {beside} the door.
The dog lay {beside} its owner and fell asleep.
examples.source: Köpek sahibinin yanında uzandı ve uyuyakaldı.

examples.target: He sat {beside} her on the park bench.
The cat sat {between} the two children.
There is a wall {between} our gardens.
The meeting is {between} 2 and 4 in the afternoon.
There is a big difference {between} the two options.
examples.source: İki seçenek arasında büyük bir fark var.

examples.target: She divided the candy {between} the two kids.
The river runs {between} the two villages.
He had to choose {between} his job and his family.
examples.source: İşi ve ailesi arasında seçim yapmak zorunda kaldı.

examples.target: The secret was kept {between} the two of them.
The bookshelf fits perfectly {between} the desk and the wall.
examples.source: Kitaplık masa ile duvar arasına mükemmel oturuyor.

examples.target: She fell asleep {between} two long chapters.
The children danced {around} the tree.
She wrapped a scarf {around} her neck.
We walked {around} the lake for an hour.
examples.source: Bir saat boyunca gölün etrafında yürüdük.

examples.target: There are many cafés {around} the main square.
examples.source: Ana meydanın etrafında birçok kafe var.

examples.target: He looked {around} but couldn't find his bag.
The earth moves {around} the sun.
She tied a ribbon {around} the gift box.
examples.source: Hediye kutusunun etrafına bir kurdele bağladı.

examples.target: They sat {around} the table and had dinner.
examples.source: Masanın etrafına oturdular ve akşam yemeği yediler.

examples.target: It takes {around} 20 minutes to get there by bus.
examples.source: Oraya otobüsle gitmek yaklaşık 20 dakika alıyor.

examples.target: He has traveled {around} the world twice.
We drove {through} the tunnel in the mountain.
She looked {through} the window at the garden.
The river flows {through} the center of the city.
He read {through} all the documents in one evening.
She learned the language {through} practice.
The light came {through} the curtains.
They walked {through} the forest to reach the village.
examples.source: Köye ulaşmak için ormanın içinden geçtiler.

examples.target: He worked {through} the night to finish the project.
examples.source: Projeyi bitirmek için gece boyunca çalıştı.

examples.target: She searched {through} her bag but found nothing.
examples.source: Çantasını aradı ama hiçbir şey bulamadı.

examples.target: We can communicate {through} the internet now.
The school is {across} the street from the park.
They swam {across} the lake.
She walked {across} the room to answer the phone.
There is a beautiful view {across} the valley.
examples.source: Vadinin karşısında güzel bir manzara var.

examples.target: He lives {across} the road from my house.
The news spread {across} the country quickly.
A smile spread {across} her face.
They looked {across} the water at the distant island.
He pushed the note {across} the table to her.
examples.source: Notu masanın üzerinden ona doğru itti.

examples.target: There are schools {across} the entire region.
She walked {toward} the door.
He moved {toward} the sound of the voice.
The storm was moving {toward} the coast.
She felt a strange pull {toward} the old house.
examples.source: Eski eve doğru garip bir çekim hissetti.

examples.target: They rode their bikes {toward} the town center.
His attitude {toward} his work changed a lot.
The child ran {toward} its mother.
She has always been kind {toward} strangers.
examples.source: Yabancılara karşı her zaman nazik olmuştur.

examples.target: We are working {toward} a better future.
examples.source: Daha iyi bir gelecek için çalışıyoruz.

examples.target: The sun was setting {toward} the west.
They walked {along} the river bank.
There are many trees planted {along} the road.
She brought her sister {along} to the party.
He sang {along} to his favorite song.
Can I come {along}?
Shops are lined up {along} the main street.
She rode her bicycle {along} the path.
He moved {along} with the crowd.
Everything is going {along} well so far.
examples.source: Şimdiye kadar her şey yolunda gidiyor.

examples.target: She carried her notebook {along} everywhere she went.
The pharmacy is {opposite} the school.
She sat {opposite} him at the meeting table.
The two friends have {opposite} personalities.
The bus stop is right {opposite} my house.
North and south are {opposite} directions.
They live {opposite} each other on the same street.
She gave the {opposite} answer to what was expected.
His behavior was {opposite} to what he had promised.
They stood {opposite} each other and shook hands.
examples.source: Birbirlerinin karşısında durdular ve el sıkıştılar.

examples.target: The bookstore is {opposite} the post office.
Please move {forward} so everyone can see.
examples.source: Herkes görebilsin diye lütfen ileri gidin.

examples.target: She leaned {forward} to hear him better.
examples.source: Onu daha iyi duymak için öne doğru eğildi.

examples.target: The army advanced {forward} through the valley.
He looked {forward} to the upcoming holiday.
She pushed the button to fast {forward} the video.
examples.source: Videoyu ileri almak için düğmeye bastı.

examples.target: Take one step {forward} and introduce yourself.
examples.source: Bir adım ileri atın ve kendinizi tanıtın.

examples.target: We need to move {forward} with the plan.
He stretched his arms {forward} to touch the wall.
examples.source: Duvara dokunmak için kollarını öne uzattı.

examples.target: The company is moving {forward} with a new strategy.
examples.source: Şirket yeni bir stratejiyle ilerlemeye devam ediyor.

examples.target: She bent {forward} and picked up the coin.
examples.source: Öne eğildi ve madeni parayı aldı.

examples.target: He took a step {backward} in surprise.
The car moved {backward} into the parking space.
examples.source: Araba park yerine geri geri girdi.

examples.target: She fell {backward} onto the soft grass.
He looked {backward} to see if anyone was following.
examples.source: Birinin onu takip edip etmediğini görmek için geriye baktı.

examples.target: The project seems to be going {backward} instead of forward.
examples.source: Proje ileri gitmek yerine geriye gidiyor gibi görünüyor.

examples.target: She could count {backward} from 100 very fast.
He glanced {backward} before crossing the road.
examples.source: Yolu geçmeden önce geriye bir göz attı.

examples.target: The child ran {backward} and bumped into the table.
examples.source: Çocuk geri geri koştu ve masaya çarptı.

examples.target: She always writes {backward} as a joke.
examples.source: Şaka olsun diye her zaman tersine yazar.

examples.target: Taking a {backward} step helped him see the full picture.
examples.source: Bir adım geriye gitmesi ona bütünü görmesini sağladı.

examples.target: She glanced {sideways} at the person beside her.
The box fell {sideways} off the shelf.
He moved {sideways} to let the others pass.
The car slid {sideways} on the icy road.
She tilted her head {sideways} to look at the picture.
examples.source: Resme bakmak için başını yana eğdi.

examples.target: He stepped {sideways} to avoid the puddle.
examples.source: Su birikintisinden kaçınmak için yana adım attı.

examples.target: The door can only open {sideways}.
She looked at him {sideways} with suspicion.
The crab walks {sideways} instead of forward.
He parked the bike {sideways} by the wall.
She found her pen {underneath} the notebook.
The cat slept {underneath} the bed.
He hid the money {underneath} the floorboard.
There is a layer of rock {underneath} the soil.
examples.source: Toprağın altında bir taş tabakası var.

examples.target: She wore a warm sweater {underneath} her coat.
examples.source: Montunun altına sıcak bir kazak giydi.

examples.target: The river flows {underneath} the bridge.
He placed a rug {underneath} the table.
The children hid {underneath} the big blanket.
Water had leaked {underneath} the door.
{Underneath} all that confidence is a shy person.
examples.source: Tüm o güvenin altında utangaç bir insan var.
